{
  "analysis": "The previous sed extraction didn't work well because objdump function sections may not be blank-line separated. I need to extract functions by address ranges and decode the rodata floating-point constants to understand the ray tracer parameters.",
  "plan": "Use nm to get function addresses, then extract disassembly by address range. Also decode all rodata floating-point constants with Python to identify scene parameters like sphere center/radius, camera position, sky coefficients.",
  "commands": [
    {
  "keystrokes": "nm /app/orig | sort
",
  "duration": 0.1
},
    {
  "keystrokes": "python3 - <<'PY'
import struct
# rodata bytes from objdump -s, starting at 0x3128
data = bytes.fromhex(
'00008040'
+'00000000000000000000000000000000'
+'00000080000000000000000000000000'
+'6f12833a000000000000000000000000'
+'ffffff7f000000000000000000000000'
+'17b7d1380000803f0000003f3333333f'
+'0000c0bfcdcc4c3e6666663fcdcccc3e'
+'000000000000f03f71fd7f4300000040'
+'0000a0c0000000bf000080bf0000c842')
base=0x3128
for i in range(0,len(data),4):
    val=struct.unpack('<f',data[i:i+4])[0]
    print(f'{base+i:04x}: {data[i:i+4].hex()} = {val}')
PY
",
  "duration": 0.1
},
    {
  "keystrokes": "grep -n '<sky_color>' /tmp/orig.asm && grep -n '<plane_intersect>' /tmp/orig.asm && grep -n '<sphere_intersect>' /tmp/orig.asm && grep -n '<trace>' /tmp/orig.asm && grep -n '<main>' /tmp/orig.asm
",
  "duration": 0.1
}
  ]
